sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Las Palmas, currently languishing in 18th place with 32 points, host Rayo Vallecano, who are in a more comfortable 8th position with 44 points, at the Gran Canaria stadium in Las Palmas de Gran Canaria. This matchup pits a struggling home side against a mid-table away team looking to solidify their standing. The referee for this game, Francisco Hernandez Maeso, averages 5.0 yellow cards and 0.17 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially card-heavy encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last encounter on November 8, 2024, Las Palmas stunned Rayo Vallecano with a 3-1 victory away from home, with Sergio Camello earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.1 Sofascore rating. Over the last 5 matchups between these teams, the record is evenly split with 2 wins each for both sides and 1 draw. Historically, head-to-head stats indicate low-scoring games, with 8 out of 10 matches featuring under 2.5 goals, and a tendency for physical play, as 5 out of 7 games saw over 4.5 cards.

sections.subtitle: Standout Players from Past Encounters
sections.text: José Campaña of Las Palmas has been a standout performer in recent games against Rayo Vallecano, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.46 over 4 starts. He has contributed 2 goals and 2 assists in these matches, highlighting his influence in midfield. Additionally, Campaña ranks 2nd in the league for aerial duels won with an 81% success rate, making him a key asset in set-piece situations.

sections.subtitle: Las Palmas Key Players and Form
sections.text: Las Palmas will rely on Sandro Ramírez in midfield, who has scored 9 goals this season against an expected goals (xG) of 3.93, overperforming by 5.07. Another player to watch is Alberto Moleiro, with 6 goals and an average rating of 6.81, though he has underperformed relative to his xG of 5.24. Up front, Oliver McBurnie has provided 6 assists despite scoring only 3 goals against an xG of 4.37. However, the team will miss key forward Fábio Silva, who has 10 goals and 3 assists, which could impact their attacking output.

sections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano Key Players and Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano’s attack will hinge on Álvaro García, who has 3 goals and 5 assists, though he has underperformed his xG of 6.28 by 3.28. Midfielder Isi Palazón adds creativity with 3 goals and 4 assists, holding a solid average rating of 6.9. Sergio Camello, with 3 goals and 2 assists, remains a threat up top, as seen in their last matchup. The absence of Pathé Ismaël Ciss, who has 4 goals, could weaken their midfield presence.

sections.subtitle: Las Palmas Season Statistics
sections.text: Las Palmas have scored 40 goals this season, ranking 10th in the league, but their defense is a concern, conceding 56 goals, placing them 19th for goals conceded. They excel in ground duels, ranking 6th with a 50.92% win rate, and interceptions per game, ranking 4th with 8.5. Their goal distribution shows strength late in games, with 21.43% of goals (13 total) scored between the 76th and 90th minutes. However, they struggle with clean sheets, managing only 4, ranking last in the league at 20th.

sections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano Goal Distribution
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano also show a knack for late goals, with 26.19% of their goals (8 total) coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. They are relatively balanced in scoring across periods, with 9 goals (9.52%) in the opening 15 minutes, showing early aggression. Their ability to score in crucial moments could be a factor against a Las Palmas side prone to conceding, as evidenced by Las Palmas being first to concede in 7 of their last 8 home games.

sections.subtitle: Team Streaks and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Las Palmas have seen both teams score in 5 of their last 7 home games, indicating defensive vulnerabilities but also attacking potential. They’ve been first half losers in 5 of their last 7 home matches, often starting poorly. Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, have played in games with under 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 away matches, suggesting a game with limited set-piece action. Las Palmas’ streak of under 4.5 cards in their last 6 home games might be tested by the referee’s high card average.
